title: Packaging smoke test is pinned to a stale tarball filename
reasoning: The packaging smoke instructions install `/tmp/codenuke-pack/codenuke-0.4.0.tgz` after running `npm pack ./apps/cli`. That filename changes whenever the CLI package version changes, so the documented smoke test will fail even when packing succeeds unless the maintainer manually updates this line for every release. The root manifest also identifies this package as only the private workspace orchestrator, so the fixed CLI tarball name is not derived from package metadata here.
recommendation: Avoid hard-coding the tarball version in the smoke command. Capture the `npm pack` output or install via a glob constrained to the pack directory, for example the single generated `codenuke-*.tgz` file.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for release packaging instructions, and this is a documentation/scripted release workflow hazard rather than runtime behavior.
minimumFixScope: Update the packaging smoke instructions in `AGENTS.md` to derive the generated tarball path instead of referencing `codenuke-0.4.0.tgz`.

history.reasoning: The original AGENTS.md evidence at lines 30-33 has been fixed: it now derives tarball with find and installs "$tarball". However, the same issue is still open elsewhere in current release documentation: apps/cli/docs/publishing.md lines 24-25 run npm pack into /tmp/codenuke-pack and then install the hard-coded /tmp/codenuke-pack/codenuke-0.4.0.tgz; line 38 also hard-codes codenuke-0.4.0.tgz for inspection. apps/cli/package.json currently reports version 0.4.0, so the command happens to match today, but it remains pinned to a versioned filename and will stale on the next CLI version bump.
commands: rg -n "\"version\"|codenuke-0\.4\.0\.tgz|tar -tf codenuke-|npm install --prefix" package.json apps/cli/package.json apps/cli/docs/publishing.md AGENTS.md
